run_metadata.coverage_summary: Attempted the full source universe (Tier 1 core government, Tier 2 broader public sector / GBE, Tier 3 private). Direct page retrieval was again fully blocked this run: WebFetch returned HTTP 403 for every external URL tested (tenders.gov.au ATM detail pages, en.wikipedia.org control host, buy.nsw, vendorpanel.com.au) and Bash curl-through-proxy was rejected with '403 CONNECT tunnel failed' for all hosts (Wikipedia, tenders.gov.au, vendorpanel). The proxy status endpoint reported no relay failures but the egress policy denies CONNECT to every external host, so no tender detail page, listing page, RSS/API endpoint or specification document could be opened. Per proxy policy, 403 org-policy denials were not retried or routed around. All capture was therefore from WebSearch (which works), cross-checked across multiple independent queries; every candidate close date was verified against at least two queries and any opportunity whose close date fell on or before 2026-07-24, or could not be corroborated, was rejected. One genuinely new currently-open opportunity was found and captured: Western Power 'Clean Energy Link' EOI for project & contract-management partners (EOI due 31 Jul 2026), corroborated by both an independent industry-news signal (reneweconomy) and Western Power's own project pages; recorded as a Tier-2 GBE tender at headline+deliverables level with the exact EOI reference and detail page still pending (Opportunities Hub is registration-gated and 403-blocked). Eight previously-seen opportunities were re-verified as still open and several were enriched with detail from cross-referenced search snippets: AZ6626/ESTS4000PH6 Hobart Class Destroyer Aegis facilities EOI (closes 17 Aug 2026, contact + 50/50 evaluation weightings recovered), PIN_2026_0334 Clean Energy Regulator ICT REOI (closes 10 Aug 2026, scope recovered), ATM_2026_6066 DCCEEW Australian Bushland Program strategic property-purchasing partners EOI (closes 3 Aug 2026, scope + criteria recovered - resolves last run's headline-only gap), ATM_2026_6786 DCCEEW GEMS verification-testing laboratory panel (closes 12 Aug 2026, deed term recovered), Health/E26-205921 Dept of Health comprehensive health services RFT (closes 28 Aug 2026, close date recovered), LCD-26-0030 Army Innovation Day 2026 RFI (closes 27 Jul 2026, closing-soon), OPC-2026-03 printing services (closes 29 Jul 2026, closing-soon), and the Transport for NSW Mittagong steel-safety-barrier RFT (scope corroborated, close date/RFT ID still unrecoverable behind the 403 wall). Two new buyer-originated source entry points were recorded. Discarded as stale/closed after date verification: JCGSCIF26 Space & Cyber Industry Forum (closed 30 Jun 2026), ATM_2026_6551 Energy Pipeline Data Services (closed 8 Jun 2026), ASCA-PITCHDAY-26-RC2 (RFI closed 14 Apr 2026), CD/RFT/27857/1 Defence Capability Support Panel (closed 5 Jan 2026), AACAP 2026-P04 (no verifiable close date). Zero tenders were fabricated.
gaps: Direct page retrieval fully blocked again this run. WebFetch returned HTTP 403 for every external URL including control host en.wikipedia.org, and Bash curl-through-proxy returned 'curl: (56) CONNECT tunnel failed, response 403' for all hosts. The org egress policy denies CONNECT to external hosts; the proxy README instructs not to retry 403/407 policy denials. Consequence: no ATM/RFT detail page, listing page, RSS/API feed or specification document could be opened; all detail is limited to cross-verified WebSearch snippets. Source-document deliverables/requirements extraction was not possible this run.

Western Power 'Clean Energy Link' EOI (project & contract-management partners): existence, buyer, scope and EOI due date (31 Jul 2026) corroborated across two independent searches (reneweconomy industry news + Western Power's own Clean Energy Link project/news pages), but the exact EOI reference number, evaluation criteria, contract value and the Opportunities Hub detail page could not be opened (registration-gated + 403). Recorded at headline+deliverables level, exact reference pending a detail pass.

Transport for NSW Mittagong network steel-safety-barrier repair/replacement RFT: scope corroborated again this run, but the RFT ID and closing date remain unrecoverable behind the 403 wall (buy.nsw detail page could not be opened). Carried forward at headline level, pending a detail pass; could not be independently re-confirmed as still open this run.

LCD-26-0030 (Army Innovation Day 2026 RFI, closes 27 Jul 2026) and OPC-2026-03 (printing services, closes 29 Jul 2026) carried forward at headline level - both close within days; full scope/documents not re-extractable behind the 403 wall.

Search snippets again recycled closed/stale opportunities as if current. Confirmed CLOSED and NOT recorded after date verification: JCGSCIF26 JCG Space & Cyber Domain Industry Forum (closed 30 Jun 2026); ATM_2026_6551 Energy Pipeline Data Services (closed 8 Jun 2026); ASCA-PITCHDAY-26-RC2 Resilient Command & Control RFI (closed 14 Apr 2026); CD/RFT/27857/1 Defence Capability Support Panel (closed 5 Jan 2026); AACAP 2026-P04 airfield design services surfaced as 'Current' but no closing date could be corroborated, so it was NOT recorded rather than risk fabrication.

Tier-1 state/territory portals (buy.nsw, Buying for Victoria, QTenders/supply.qld, Tenders WA, Tenders SA, Tenders Tasmania, NT QTOL, ACT Tenders): reachable only as landing pages via search; individual open tenders could not be drilled into or date-verified behind the 403 wall, so apart from the carried-forward TfNSW Mittagong item no NEW state opportunity could be confirmed as published in the last 7 days at a trustworthy level of detail.

Tier-3 corporate (SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network, plus BHP, Rio Tinto, Fortescue, Woodside, Santos, Coles, Woolworths, Qantas, banks, telcos): checked as required. No publicly-advertised Australian RFx was visible without a supplier login; corporate opportunities remain registration-gated or invitation-only, so none were captured as tenders. The relevant corporate portals are already recorded in prior runs' discovered_sources. Ausgrid (BESS Tranche 2 vendor identification) and Transgrid (1GW grid-forming battery tender) were noted as active GBE market activity but their specific opportunity pages are TenderLink/Ariba registration-gated and could not be date-verified.

Deferred to a future run with working page retrieval: additional current 2026 AusTender IDs surfaced only by reference (AACAP 2026-P04 airfield design, RFT 1001032028, JASD/RFT/20200/1, 77680) could not be titled/dated/scoped without opening the 403-blocked detail pages, so they were not recorded rather than risk fabrication.
recommended_seed_additions: Western Power - Tenders and Registrations of Interest (Opportunities Hub): buyer-originated GBE entry point for WA transmission/network EOIs and RFTs, distinct from the already-known SAP-Ariba supplier onboarding page. https://www.westernpower.com.au/resources-education/suppliers/tenders-and-registrations-of-interest/ (registration required for full detail).

LGANT Current Tenders (Local Government Association of the Northern Territory) - TenderLink-hosted aggregation of NT council tender notices posted by the councils themselves: https://portal.tenderlink.com/lgant/alltenders/ - useful NT local-government coverage not captured by the core NT QTOL portal.

tenders.source_name: Western Power - Tenders and Registrations of Interest (Opportunities Hub)
tenders.title: Clean Energy Link - Project & Contract Management Partners (Expression of Interest)
tenders.category: Project and contract management services / transmission infrastructure
tenders.description: Western Power is seeking expressions of interest from organisations able to provide project and contract-management resources to help deliver its Clean Energy Link program - a set of projects to upgrade and expand WA's South West Interconnected System so it can safely connect and transport increasing levels of renewable energy.
tenders.deliverables_summary: The successful respondents would provide project and contract-management partner services across the Clean Energy Link program, covering construction, safety and health oversight and monitoring; contract-management administration; schedule and cost management; engineering and interface coordination; and change management and reporting, in support of delivering major high-capacity transmission line and related network infrastructure.
key_deliverables: Construction, safety and health oversight and monitoring
tenders.scope_of_work: Provision of project and contract-management partner resources to assist delivery of major high-capacity transmission line and related infrastructure projects within Western Power's Clean Energy Link program (expansion/upgrade of the SWIS to connect renewable energy).
tenders.eligibility: Open to suitably experienced project- and contract-management organisations; response and full detail via Western Power's registration-gated Opportunities Hub.
tenders.submission_method: Via Western Power Opportunities Hub (registration required)
tenders.detail_source: Cross-verified WebSearch snippets: reneweconomy.com.au 'Network seeks partners on upgrade to nation's largest isolated grid' + Western Power Clean Energy Link project/news pages (westernpower.com.au). Opportunities Hub detail page 403-blocked / registration-gated.
tenders.reasoning: Discovered via a Tier-2 GBE energy/utilities sweep; industry-news aggregator used only as a discovery signal, then pivoted to the buyer's own Opportunities Hub as the source of truth. Buyer-originated (Western Power).
tenders.notes: Existence, buyer, scope and EOI due date (31 Jul 2026) corroborated across two independent sources. Exact EOI reference number, evaluation criteria and contract value could not be read because the Opportunities Hub is registration-gated and all pages returned 403 this run. Headline + deliverables captured; exact reference pending a detail pass.

tenders.title: ESTS4000PH6 - Facilities to Support Hobart Class Destroyer (DDG) Aegis Combat System Enhancement (Invitation to Register Interest)
tenders.category: Building construction and infrastructure / defence estate works
tenders.description: First-stage Invitation to Register Interest for the design/delivery of facilities supporting the Hobart Class Destroyer (DDG) Aegis Combat System Enhancement (SEA 4000 Phase 6), including modified and expanded training facilities and working accommodation at Navy Training Systems Centre, Randwick, NSW.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Delivery of new and refurbished infrastructure and capital-build facilities - modified and expanded training facilities and working accommodation, including construction of new and refurbished training facilities at Navy Training Systems Centre, Randwick Barracks, NSW - to support SEA 4000 Phase 6 across NSW and SA. This ITR is stage one of a two-stage selection process.
key_deliverables: New and refurbished training facilities at Navy Training Systems Centre, Randwick
Capital-build facilities supporting the Aegis Combat System Enhancement
tenders.scope_of_work: Facilities to support SEA 4000 Phase 6 in New South Wales and South Australia; two-stage procurement beginning with an Invitation to Register Interest.
tenders.eligibility: Open (Invitation to Register Interest); respondents shortlisted for stage two.
tenders.delivery_location: Navy Training Systems Centre, Randwick, NSW (and SA)
tenders.detail_source: Cross-verified WebSearch snippets of the AusTender AZ6626 ATM view and DECnet project pages; detail page itself 403-blocked.
tenders.reasoning: Re-verified from a Commonwealth defence-estate sweep; close date, contact and 50/50 evaluation weightings recovered this run.
tenders.notes: Close date 17 Aug 2026 12:00pm ACT and evaluation weightings corroborated across two searches. Still open. Detail page could not be opened directly (403); tender documents not accessible.

tenders.title: Proof of Concept ICT Capability for Regulatory and Register(s) Functions (Request for Expressions of Interest)
tenders.description: Stage-1 Request for Expressions of Interest seeking suppliers with relevant experience to support the Clean Energy Regulator's current and future regulatory and registry functions through ICT capability; used to shortlist respondents for a subsequent Proof of Concept (PoC) procurement in Stage 2.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Respondents describe market capability, solution approaches and implementation considerations for an ICT capability supporting the Regulator's regulatory and register(s) functions. This REOI (Stage 1) shortlists suppliers to participate in a Stage-2 Proof of Concept procurement.
key_deliverables: Shortlisting input for a subsequent Proof of Concept (Stage 2)
tenders.scope_of_work: Two-stage procurement; this REOI is Stage 1, informing a Stage-2 Proof of Concept of ICT capability for regulatory and register(s) functions.
tenders.eligibility: Open to suppliers with relevant regulatory/registry ICT experience and capability.
tenders.briefing: Optional industry briefing was held 2:00pm (ACT) Tuesday 21 July 2026 (registration closed 20 July 2026) - now past.
tenders.detail_source: Cross-verified WebSearch snippets of the AusTender PIN_2026_0334 ATM view; detail page 403-blocked.
tenders.reasoning: Re-verified from a Commonwealth ICT sweep; scope, two-stage structure and briefing details recovered this run.
tenders.notes: Close date 10 Aug 2026 5:00pm ACT confirmed. Still open. Industry briefing already held. Detail page could not be opened directly (403).

tenders.title: Australian Bushland Program - Strategic Property Purchasing Partners (Expression of Interest)
tenders.buyer: Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water (DCCEEW)
tenders.description: Expression of Interest for strategic property-purchasing partners to support the Australian Bushland Program, with locations across ACT, NSW, VIC, SA, WA, QLD, NT and TAS. A multi-stage tender.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Partners to assist DCCEEW with strategic property purchasing under the Australian Bushland Program across all states/territories, delivered under a multi-stage arrangement assessed against contract delivery, experience, specified personnel and compliance criteria.
key_deliverables: Strategic property-purchasing partner services under the Australian Bushland Program
Delivery across ACT, NSW, VIC, SA, WA, QLD, NT and TAS
tenders.scope_of_work: Multi-stage EOI to establish strategic property-purchasing partners for the Australian Bushland Program.
tenders.delivery_location: ACT, NSW, VIC, SA, WA, QLD, NT, TAS
tenders.detail_source: Cross-verified WebSearch snippets of the AusTender ATM_2026_6066 ATM view; detail page 403-blocked.
tenders.reasoning: Previously headline-only; the Australian Bushland Program scope, publish date, locations and evaluation criteria were recovered this run, resolving last run's pending-detail gap.
tenders.notes: Close date 3 Aug 2026 2:00pm ACT confirmed. Program identified as the Australian Bushland Program strategic property purchasing. Still open. Detail page could not be opened directly (403).

tenders.title: GEMS Verification Testing Laboratory Panel
tenders.buyer: Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water (DCCEEW)
tenders.description: Establishment of a panel of appropriately accredited testing laboratories to undertake verification testing of products regulated under the Greenhouse and Energy Minimum Standards Act 2012 (GEMS Act).
tenders.deliverables_summary: Accredited testing laboratories to perform verification testing of GEMS-regulated products, supporting the Department's compliance and enforcement functions under the GEMS Act 2012. The resulting deed is anticipated to run from 1 November 2026 to 30 June 2030.
key_deliverables: Accredited laboratory services under a panel deed
tenders.scope_of_work: Panel of accredited laboratories for GEMS Act verification testing; deed anticipated 1 Nov 2026 to 30 Jun 2030.
requirements: Appropriate laboratory accreditation for the relevant product testing
tenders.eligibility: Open to appropriately accredited testing laboratories.
tenders.detail_source: Cross-verified WebSearch snippets of the AusTender ATM_2026_6786 ATM view; detail page 403-blocked.
tenders.reasoning: Re-verified from a Commonwealth environment sweep; deed term (1 Nov 2026 - 30 Jun 2030) and accreditation requirement recovered this run.
tenders.notes: Close date 12 Aug 2026 2:00pm ACT confirmed. Still open. Detail page could not be opened directly (403).

tenders.title: Comprehensive Health Services - ICT Design and Implementation (Request for Tender)
tenders.buyer: Department of Health, Disability and Ageing
tenders.description: Request for Tender under which the service provider is to design and implement services, including implementation planning, design, build, testing, data migration, deployment and IT support services, in the comprehensive health services category.
tenders.deliverables_summary: The service provider is to design and implement services covering implementation planning, design, build, testing, data migration, deployment and ongoing IT support, delivering a comprehensive health services capability for the Department.
tenders.scope_of_work: Design and implementation of services including implementation planning, design, build, testing, data migration, deployment and IT support (comprehensive health services, UNSPSC 85100000).
tenders.detail_source: Cross-verified WebSearch snippets of the AusTender Health/E26-205921 ATM view; detail page 403-blocked.
tenders.reasoning: Re-verified from a Commonwealth health/ICT sweep; the closing date (28 Aug 2026), publish date and category were recovered this run.
tenders.notes: Close date 28 Aug 2026 2:00pm ACT confirmed across two searches. Still open. Exact solution scope (which health service/system) not readable behind the 403 wall; captured as ICT design & implementation per the published notice text.

tenders.title: Army Innovation Day 2026 (AID26) - Request for Information
tenders.description: Request for Information supporting Army Innovation Day 2026 (AID26), seeking industry input on innovative capabilities.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Industry responses to an RFI informing Army Innovation Day 2026; captured at headline level this run.
tenders.reasoning: Carried forward from prior runs; still listed as a current AusTender ATM, closing 27 Jul 2026.
tenders.notes: Closes 27 Jul 2026 (within days). Carried at headline level; full scope not re-extractable behind the 403 wall this run.

tenders.title: Print on Demand of the Federal Register of Legislation / Printing Services
tenders.description: Request for Tender for printing services (print-on-demand of the Federal Register of Legislation) for the Office of Parliamentary Counsel.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Provision of printing / print-on-demand services for the Federal Register of Legislation; captured at headline level this run.
tenders.reasoning: Carried forward from prior runs; still listed as a current AusTender ATM, closing 29 Jul 2026.
tenders.notes: Closes 29 Jul 2026 (within days). Carried at headline level; full scope not re-extractable behind the 403 wall this run.

tenders.title: Mittagong Network - Steel Safety Barrier Repair and Replacement (RFT)
tenders.description: Request for Tender for inspection, repair and replacement of damaged guardrail and wire-rope steel safety barriers within the Mittagong road network (NSW Southern Highlands).
tenders.deliverables_summary: Inspection, repair and replacement of damaged guardrail and wire-rope steel safety barriers within the Mittagong road network. Captured at headline level; the detail page could not be opened this run.
key_deliverables: Inspection of steel safety barriers in the Mittagong network
Repair of damaged guardrail and wire-rope barriers
tenders.scope_of_work: Inspection, repair and replacement of damaged guardrail and wire-rope steel safety barriers within the Mittagong road network.
tenders.reasoning: Carried forward from the prior run; scope corroborated again across buy.nsw search snippets, but detail page remained 403-blocked.
tenders.notes: RFT ID and closing date remain unrecoverable behind the 403 wall; could not be independently re-confirmed as still open this run. Carried at headline level pending a detail pass.

discovered_sources.source_name: Western Power - Tenders and Registrations of Interest (Opportunities Hub)
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier-2 GBE energy/utilities sweep for open EOIs; found Western Power's Clean Energy Link EOI referenced via industry news, then located Western Power's own Tenders & Registrations of Interest page as the buyer-originated entry point (distinct from the already-known SAP-Ariba supplier onboarding page).
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated GBE portal for WA transmission/network EOIs and RFTs. Full opportunity detail is registration-gated; page returned 403 this run so listings could not be enumerated directly.

discovered_sources.source_name: LGANT Current Tenders (Local Government Association of the Northern Territory)
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Local-government / VendorPanel-and-council sweep surfaced an LGANT TenderLink-hosted current-tenders portal aggregating NT council notices posted by the councils themselves.
discovered_sources.notes: NT local-government coverage not captured by the core NT QTOL portal. Buyer-originated (council notices via the LGA's TenderLink portal). Could not be opened directly this run (403).

discovered_sources.source_name: SAP Business Network Discovery (SAP Ariba Discovery)
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Mandatory Tier-3 check. No publicly-advertised Australian RFx was visible without a supplier login this run.
discovered_sources.notes: Largest single slice of corporate opportunity; individual postings are registration-gated. Recorded to keep the corporate source set complete; no opportunity captured (login-gated).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier-2 GBE energy sweep; Ausgrid noted as running a BESS Tranche 2 vendor-identification exercise, but the specific opportunity page is registration-gated and could not be date-verified.
discovered_sources.notes: Known GBE source; re-checked this run. Opportunity detail login-gated / 403-blocked, so no tender captured.
